checklist.evidence: Orphan-branch-creation race: both writers read old=""; git rejects the loser with 'reference already exists', which does not match the *"but expected"* retry arm, so the loop breaks after one attempt and the caller's commit is lost. Reproduced twice in a scratch repo.

_git_show returns None for both 'blob absent' and 'git failed'; the caller continues on both, collapsing an unestablished read onto a measured absence — the very fail-open the comment block directly above it describes preventing.

stale_prose_check.note: R3 count-locked, docs/DEVFLOW_SYSTEM_OVERVIEW.md:573 — informational, never gates

phase3_findings.description: bash 3.2 empty-array expansion under set -u aborts commit_on's orphan-root path: the telemetry branch can NEVER be created on stock macOS bash, silently (exit 0) with a misattributed 'object-store write failed' breadcrumb.

CAS-race discriminator string-matches git stderr for 'but expected'; the branch-creation race ('reference already exists') skips the retry, drops telemetry, and the breadcrumb asserts 'NOT a concurrent writer'.
devflow_telemetry_list_blobs fails open and silent: an unreadable tree on a PRESENT ref returns empty output, emptying the fix-commit exclusion set → double-counted telemetry.
Retry/abandon decisions parse localized (gettext-translated) git prose.
telemetry.branch never validated as a ref name; a bad config value is reported as a lock/read-only/disk fault, on every run.
mkdir rc for .devflow/tmp discarded → the cloud-sandbox write denial is misreported as 'object-store write failed'; an absent staging root reads as a clean no-op.
_DEVFLOW_TELEMETRY_BRANCH_SOURCED sentinel set at the top of the file: witnesses 'source began', not 'source succeeded'. Latent fail-open; the comment claiming otherwise is false.
Push loop entered on the NOOP path: the local Stop hook made a blocking network push on every agent turn.
Retry fetch uses a non-forced refspec into a remote-tracking ref; origin is hard-coded while the no-remote guard accepts any remote name; _telemetry_branch ignores DEVFLOW_CONFIG_FILE.

review-and-fix/SKILL.md self-contradicts (938 vs 968) about the chore: persist commit; review/SKILL.md:238/:1339 still describe a .devflow/logs/efficiency/ file write; phase-3-review.md + docs/efficiency-trace.md still describe the deleted source=="review" skip and falsely claim a failed persist leaves the record on disk.
TB_SP stages only non-conforming paths, so the staged-path guard's filter-not-abort semantics are untested; a whole-batch-abort regression ships green.
recorded_fix_shas' telemetry-blob failure breadcrumb is unreachable because a pipeline's rc is the last command's rc.
AC19's workflow audit globs *.yml only; GitHub Actions also honors *.yaml, so a *.yaml workflow with an unfiltered on: push would be invisible to an audit asserting 'every workflow'.

fix_decisions.claim_text: recorded_fix_shas' 'unreadable telemetry blob' breadcrumb is dead code, because a pipeline's rc is the last command's rc, so a devflow_telemetry_show_blob failure is masked by jq exiting 0.
fix_decisions.evidence: REFUTED. `set -euo pipefail` is set at lib/efficiency-trace.sh:48, and shell options are inherited by the $(...) subshell, so the pipeline's rc IS show_blob's failure and the warn arm DOES fire. Reproduced directly: a script with `set -euo pipefail; show_blob() { return 1; }; if ! out="$(show_blob | jq -r '.x' 2>/dev/null)"; then echo FIRED; fi` prints FIRED. The finding's premise (no pipefail) is false against HEAD.

verification_evidence.note: 5713 passed, 0 failed (baseline 5698 → +15). shellcheck clean, ruff clean. Mutation-checked: reverting the guarded array expansion reproduces Critical-1 (no branch created under bash 3.2); reverting the CAS ref-re-read reproduces the lost write + the 'NOT a concurrent writer' misattribution. Static array pin: 0 on the real file, 1 under a reintroduction mutation.
